name: Old Fashioned Buttermilk Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450°F; set rack in middle level.
2. Prepare biscuit dough.
a. Combine the flours, salt, and baking powder in a mixing bowl and stir well to mix.
b. Rub the butter into the dry mixture by hand or with a pastry blender until the mixture is crumbly.
c. Stir in 3/4 cup of buttermilk (or heavy cream) with a fork, continuing to stir gently until the dough begins to hold together. If the dough is too dry, add more buttermilk, about 1 tablespoon at a time.
d. Sprinkle the work surface generously with flour and turn out the dough onto it. Fold the dough over on itself two or three times. Use the dough immediately.
3. Pat the dough into a 6-by-12-inch rectangle. Cut into eight 3-inch biscuits using a sharp, floured knife.
4. Transfer the biscuits to a parchment-lined cookie sheet.
5. Brush tops of the biscuits with buttermilk (or heavy cream).
6. Bake for 10-15 minutes, or until well risen and golden brown.
